{"query":"LVMH Pride Month 2026 global employee engagement diversity inclusion initiatives July 8","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1464,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.lvmh.com/en/news-lvmh/lvmh-celebrates-pride-month-2026-with-the-mobilization-of-its-employees-worldwide","title":"LVMH celebrates Pride Month 2026 with the mobiliza... - LVMH","content":"Throughout Pride Month 2026, LVMH and our Maisons came together to celebrate and continue building a more inclusive environment for all.
Once again this year, thousands of LVMH employees mobilized across 12 countries/regions around the world, taking part in a series of events.
From awareness-raising initiatives to the festive celebrations of Pride marches - notably in Tokyo, Madrid, New York, Los Angeles, Amsterdam, Taiwan, Seoul, and Barcelona - this collective momentum demonstrated the strength of the Group’s commitment on an international scale.
That is something I am truly proud to be part of”, reminds Joseph Huang from All LVMH Pride TAIWAN."},{"url":"https://www.linkedin.com/posts/lvmh_lvmh-pride-activity-7467153602071973889-35lb","title":"#lvmh #pride | LVMH | 11 comments","content":"this Is More Than a T-Shirt On the occasion of Pride Month 2026, LVMH is strengthening its commitment to LGBTI+ inclusion and unveiling a new edition of its Pride T-shirt, designed this year by Nicolas Ghesquière, Artistic Director of Louis Vuitton Women’s Collections.
At LVMH, LGBTI+ inclusion is part of a long-term approach led across the Group and its Maisons through awareness-raising, training, support initiatives, and the ongoing mobilization of teams and internal networks.
More than a T-shirt, a symbol of unity Worn during Pride walks in cities including Tokyo, Madrid, New York, Los Angeles, Amsterdam, Taiwan, Bangkok, Seoul, and Barcelona, this T-shirt will accompany the mobilization of thousands of employees.
The responsibility to build the culture of "fashion diplomacy" falls on heritage houses like Chanel, Loro Piana, and especially Dior.
Palmieri From Brooklyn Street Vendor to Manhattan Luxury Real Estate For more than four decades, Joseph Aquino has built a career representing some of the world’s most prestigious luxury retailers and property owners throughout New York City."},{"url":"https://www.generali.com/info/discovering-generali/all/2026/Pride-Month-2026","title":"Pride Month 2026 - Generali Group","content":"The Group is expanding its participation in international Pride celebrations, supporting LGBTQIA+ communities across multiple countries through public engagement in Pride events.
We PROUD plays an essential role in fostering an inclusive workplace culture, helping raise awareness of the challenges faced by LGBTQIA+ employees and strengthening understanding of inclusion across the organisation.
Through initiatives and projects aligned with the Generali People Strategy “GPeople27 – People Powered Excellence”, the community contributes to removing cultural and behavioural barriers, while supporting meaningful dialogue on diversity, equity and inclusion."}]}

At LVMH, Pride Month is not a one-month initiative. We kicked off with “This Is More Than a T-Shirt,” a T-shirt designed by Nicolas Ghesquière, Artistic Director of Louis Vuitton Women’s Collections, and proudly embraced by our teams worldwide. Throughout Pride Month 2026, LVMH and our Maisons came together to celebrate and continue building a more inclusive environment for all. For this 2026 edition, a powerful symbol of the Group’s commitment was the Pride T-shirt designed by Nicolas Ghesquière, Artistic Director of Louis Vuitton Women’s Collections, inspired by the work of Keith Haring. Pride Month 2026 thus highlighted the strength of this collective dynamic, driven by its employees, the All LVMH Pride network, the Maisons, and all allies mobilized around the world.
